bibliographic_data.title: Successive Cancellation Decoding of Polar Codes using Stochastic Computing
bibliographic_data.abstract: Polar codes have emerged as the most favorable channel codes for their unique capacity-achieving property. To date, numerous works have been reported for efficient design of polar codes decoder. However, these prior efforts focused on design of polar decoders via deterministic computation, while the behavior of stochastic polar decoder, which can have potential advantages such as low complexity and strong error-resilience, has not been studied in existing literatures. This paper, for the first time, investigates polar decoding using stochastic logic. Specifically, the commonly-used successive cancellation (SC) algorithm is reformulated into the stochastic form. Several methods that can potentially improve decoding performance are discussed and analyzed. Simulation results show that a stochastic SC decoder can achieve similar error-correcting performance as its deterministic counterpart. This work can pave the way for future hardware design of stochastic polar codes decoders.

core_content.novelty_claim: This paper investigates the use of stochastic computing in successive cancellation (SC) decoding of polar codes, which has not been studied before and can offer advantages such as low complexity and strong error-resilience.
core_content.key_methodology: The deterministic SC algorithm is reformulated into a stochastic form. Several methods to potentially improve decoding performance are discussed and analyzed.
outcomes: A stochastic SC decoder can achieve similar error-correcting performance as its deterministic counterpart.
This work paves the way for future hardware design of stochastic polar codes decoders.
core_content.full_text_markdown: Polar codes have emerged as the most favorable channel codes due to their unique capacity-achieving property. However, prior works on polar decoding focused on deterministic computation, while stochastic computing offers potential advantages such as low complexity and strong error-resilience. This paper investigates the use of stochastic logic in successive cancellation (SC) decoding of polar codes for the first time. The deterministic SC algorithm is reformulated into a stochastic form, and several methods to improve decoding performance are analyzed. Simulation results show that the stochastic SC decoder can achieve similar error-correcting performance as its deterministic counterpart. This work paves the way for future hardware design of stochastic polar code decoders.
